## touch-browser v0.2.0

This release makes the CLI and MCP package more directly usable by AI agents. The main change is an explicit agent contract: agents can now ask the runtime what it supports, request compact JSON output, receive structured recovery hints, and decide whether evidence is safe to reuse without parsing free-form text.

### Added

- `touch-browser capabilities` / `touch-browser status` for AI-facing runtime discovery.
- Global `--agent-json` compact output with `agentContract`, `nextActions`, `reuseSummary`, `sessionFile`, and compact evidence/session fields.
- Global `--json-errors` structured error payloads with `retryable` and `suggestedAction`.
- Evidence `reuseAllowed` signals for high-confidence supported claims.
- Evidence `primarySupportSnippet` and `supportSnippets[].supportRole` fields so agents can distinguish primary support from context.
- Published `@nangman-infra/touch-browser-mcp@0.2.0` metadata aligned with the `v0.2.0` GitHub Release assets.

### Changed

- Bumped Rust workspace, CLI runtime, MCP bridge, npm package, server descriptors, Docker install pin, and Glama metadata to `0.2.0`.
- Regenerated evidence golden fixtures for the new support snippet contract.
- Updated CLI surface documentation for the agent-oriented output contract.

### Fixed

- Fixed generated fixture formatting for the lint gate.
- Relaxed a CI-only Playwright coverage timeout.
- Adjusted Sonar coverage scope while Rust coverage is not generated.
- Reduced the remaining Sonar cognitive complexity issue in the agent contract compaction path.

### Verification

- Main SonarQube workflow passed for `5760f55`.
- Standalone release workflow passed for `v0.2.0`.
- macOS arm64 and Linux x86_64 standalone bundles plus checksums are attached to this release.
- npm `latest` points to `@nangman-infra/touch-browser-mcp@0.2.0`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/nangman-infra/touch-browser/compare/v0.1.13...v0.2.0
